skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to review UI, check accessibility, audit design, review UX, or check my site against guidelines.
skill.summary: Review UI code for web interface guidelines compliance, accessibility, and UX best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The backticks flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' are markdown code formatting. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are scanner errors on random markdown text. The network URL is a legitimate public GitHub resource (vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines) intentionally fetched to get current guidelines. This is a simple skill that fetches public guidelines and reviews UI code for compliance.

medium_findings.title: Backtick Detection - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged markdown backticks as Ruby/shell execution. These are code formatting in markdown documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks surround 'file:line' format description and URL - standard markdown syntax, not code execution

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - Intentional Design
low_findings.description: URL to fetch web interface guidelines from public GitHub repository
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is intentional design - fetching current guidelines from vercel-labs public repo for compliance review

content.value_statement: This skill automatically reviews UI code against web interface guidelines to ensure accessibility, usability, and design consistency. It fetches the latest guidelines and provides actionable feedback.
actual_capabilities: Fetch latest web interface guidelines from public GitHub repository
Review UI code files against accessibility and design guidelines
Check for compliance with web interface best practices
Output findings in actionable file:line format
Support pattern-based file review for bulk checking
Provide accessibility and UX improvement suggestions
limitations: Does not render or preview actual visual output
Cannot execute code or test in browser
Relies on external guideline fetch - requires network
Reviews code structure only, not runtime behavior

use_cases.title: Web Developer Code Review
use_cases.description: A web developer wants to ensure their UI components follow accessibility and design best practices before deployment.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audit
use_cases.description: A UX designer needs to verify that all pages meet accessibility standards and WCAG guidelines.

use_cases.title: Design System Compliance
use_cases.description: A team lead wants to ensure all team members follow consistent design patterns in their implementations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic UI Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-design-guidelines skill to review my UI code in [filename] for compliance with web interface guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick single-file review when user provides a specific file

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines to check [file or pattern] for accessibility compliance and suggest improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused accessibility audit with specific emphasis on a11y

prompt_templates.title: Full Design Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines to perform a comprehensive review of [directory or pattern]. Check all files for web interface guidelines compliance, accessibility, and UX best practices.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete audit of multiple files or entire project

prompt_templates.title: Pattern-Based Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines skill to review all [*.jsx or specific pattern] files in [directory] for web interface guidelines compliance.

output_examples.output:
Button component review complete:

✓ Passed: Button has proper semantic HTML structure
✓ Passed: Sufficient color contrast (4.5:1 ratio)
⚠ Warning: Missing aria-label for icon-only buttons at line 23
⚠ Warning: Focus states could be more visible at line 45

Recommendation: Add aria-label="Submit" to icon-only button elements for screen reader support.

output_examples.input: Check index.html for accessibility compliance using web-design-guidelines
output_examples.output:
Accessibility audit for index.html:

✓ Passed: Valid document structure with semantic elements
✓ Passed: Lang attribute present
⚠ Warning: Image at line 15 missing alt attribute
⚠ Warning: Form labels missing at lines 30-35
⚠ Warning: Skip navigation link recommended

Action required: Add alt text to all images and associate labels with form inputs.

best_practices: Fetch the latest guidelines before each review to ensure current best practices
Review complete files rather than snippets for accurate context
Address accessibility warnings first as they impact the widest audience
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for backend logic or API code review
Avoid reviewing generated or minified code - the skill needs readable source
Do not expect visual rendering - this is code analysis only

faq.question: What guidelines does this skill check against?
faq.answer: It fetches the latest web interface guidelines from vercel-labs web-interface-guidelines repository on GitHub.

faq.question: Can this skill check my live website?
faq.answer: No, this skill reviews source code files only. It cannot access live websites or render pages.

faq.question: Does it check for specific frameworks like React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes, it reviews the code structure and applies generic web guidelines. Framework-specific checks depend on the fetched guidelines content.

faq.question: How do I provide files for review?
faq.answer: Pass a file path, directory, or glob pattern as an argument to the skill.

faq.question: What if no files are specified?
faq.answer: The skill will ask you to provide which files or patterns you want reviewed.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and analyzes your code. It provides recommendations but does not make changes.
